original_abstract: The paper presents an analysis of the spatial structure of the Polish agriculture. Basing on the results of the Agricultural Census of 2010, six groups of internal factors in agriculture have been differentiated (land quality and land use; agrarian structure; socio-demographic factors; technical equipment; production structure; production and economical factors), which facilitated both the creation of the index of the general level of agriculture and the evaluation of the agricultural structure. The spatial matrices which evolved in the process were juxtaposed with the selected external factors in agriculture: natural, historical, urban and those related to the financial support available within the Common Agricultural Policy. It has been shown that the diverse spatial structure of agriculture is connected with natural factors to a small extent only; within the category of man-made factors, even if the European funds are well absorbed, historical determinants are the most significant.
humanized_abstract: The paper carries out the analysis of the spatial structure of Polish agriculture, on the basis of the Agricultural Census of 2010. On the basis of interrelations between the individual groups of variables from six groups of internal factors of agriculture: land quality and land use; agrarian structure; socio-demographic factors; technical equipment; production structure; production and economical factors, it was possible to create the index of the general level of agriculture and evaluate the agricultural structure, using spatial matrices made on their basis, which were confronted with a set of selected external factors of agriculture: natural, historical, urban and relating to the financial support, within the framework of the Common Agricultural Policy. The diversification of the agricultural structure is carried out only to a small extent by the natural factors. In the man-made factors group, even if the EU funds are fully utilized, it is the historical determinants which are the most significant.

On the basis of interrelations between the individual groups of variables from six groups of internal factors of agriculture: land quality and land use; agrarian structure; socio-demographic factors; technical equipment; production structure; production and economical factors, it was possible to create the index of the general level of agriculture and evaluate the agricultural structure, using spatial matrices made on their basis, which were confronted with a set of selected external factors of agriculture: natural, historical, urban and relating to the financial support, within the framework of the Common Agricultural Policy. The diversification of the agricultural structure is carried out only to a small extent by the natural factors. In the man-made factors group, even if the EU funds are fully utilized, it is the historical determinants which are the most significant.
